extraction_note: Two ingestion paths in extract_web.py: (1) DBAASP REST JSON peptide cards via numeric ID walk (list pagination on dbaasp.org returns duplicate pages); (2) DRAMP antimicrobial-activity bulk Excel. DBAASP rows map publication_year from articles[], source_url from PubMed when PMID is linked, else peptide card URL. DRAMP rows map publication_year from Reference, source_url from Pubmed_ID, doi from Reference when present. Dependencies: requests, pandas, openpyxl.

input_pages.robots_notes: Card JSON at GET /peptides/{id}?format=json. ID walk probes totalCount once; throttle ≥1 req/s. Legacy list_pages mode kept but known broken (duplicate pages).
input_pages.selection_reason: Primary structured MIC per peptide–target with medium/inoculum when available.

parser_plan.filter: Monomers only; MIC activity measure; bacterial targets.
parser_plan.notes: MIC concentration text through extraction; canonical units via scripts/utils.py. clean_dataset coerces measurement_value to numeric scalars in data/processed/dataset.csv.

input_pages.snapshot_note: ID-walk run summary (ids_attempted, cards_fetched, rows_written) or legacy list page index.

input_pages.access_method: Download `Antimicrobial_amps.xlsx` from DRAMP 4.0 bulk downloads (https://dramp.cpu-bioinfor.org/downloads/; legacy path segment `DRAMP3.0_new` in the direct URL). Save unchanged copy as local_workbook_path `data/raw/web/Antimicrobial.xlsx`. Columns include DRAMP_ID, Sequence, Name, Source, Activity, Target_Organism, Pubmed_ID.
input_pages.notes: publication_year parsed from Reference; source_url from Pubmed_ID; row doi from Reference when present (database DOI kept in notes).

parser_plan.filter: Activity contains Antibacterial; Target_Organism must contain `(MIC …)` clauses parseable by shared regex helpers.
